TransponderChannelData.title: Satellite Frequencies

errorMsgs.NoChannelInfo: Satellite does not have channel information
errorMsgs.NotSatellite: Selected object is not a satellite
errorMsgs.FetchFailed: Failed to fetch channel info for {name} and {altName}

help.overview: Transponder and Channel Info shows what a geostationary TV or communications satellite is actually broadcasting: every known channel with its frequency, beam, transmission system, and encryption. The data covers a few hundred well known broadcast satellites and is fetched live when you open the menu.
help.imgAlt: The Satellite Frequencies table for a broadcast satellite
help.imgCaption: Each row is one channel with its beam, frequency, system, and encryption details.
help.columns:
• TV Channel: the broadcast service name.
  • Beam: the satellite antenna beam carrying the channel, which determines the coverage footprint.
  • Frequency: the downlink frequency and polarization.
  • System and SR/FEC: the transmission standard (for example DVB-S2) with its symbol rate and forward error correction.
  • Video and Language: codec and audio languages.
  • Encryption: whether the channel is free to air or scrambled, and with what system.
help.howToUse:
1. Select a broadcast satellite; the bottom icon enables only for satellites with known channel data.
  2. Open the menu from the bottom bar or press T. The channel list downloads and fills the table.
  3. Use the download button in the title bar to export the table as a spreadsheet.
help.tip1: The supported satellites are registered as a TV Satellites constellation in the Constellations menu, which is the quickest way to find one to inspect.
help.tip2: Channel data comes from the KeepTrack API and reflects public listings; it requires an internet connection.

TransponderChannelData.attribution: Channel data from the KeepTrack API (public listings).
